claim.observation: windows.netscan shows PID 22456 'subject_srv.ex' LISTENING on 0.0.0.0:3262 (TCPv4 offset 0xbbe3f620, plus the matching TCPv6 ::3262), and an ESTABLISHED TCPv4 session 172.16.6.15:3262 with foreign address 172.16.5.50:43036 (offset 0xbbf88a30) owned by the same PID 22456.
claim.interpretation: A process named subject_srv.exe is bound to an uncommon TCP port (3262) and has a live inbound connection from another internal host, 172.16.5.50. The remote side's high ephemeral source port (43036) terminating on local port 3262 is consistent with 172.16.5.50 acting as the CLIENT of a custom service running on this host -- an internal peer that reached this listener over the network. A custom listening service of this kind can serve as a remote-access channel, and it is the named subject of this case, so it warrants follow-up. This netscan output alone evidences only the live socket and its single internal peer; it does not by itself characterise the traffic carried over the session as malicious.
